question: Is the original hand and phone body from the first input image preserved, maintaining the same pose and holding position?
Is the phone screen content replaced with a pixel art game interface, strictly confined within the screen boundaries without bleeding onto the bezel?
Is the background of the interface recognizable as the city skyline from the fourth input image (Empire State Building/Freedom Tower)?
Has the city skyline background been pixelated to a low-resolution style as requested?
Is the background color palette shifted to a cyan-purple gaming atmosphere?
Is the pixel art character from the second input image (green hooded figure with sword) visible in the center of the screen?
Is the 3D glassy button from the third input image placed at the bottom of the screen to serve as a 'Start' button?
Does the 'Start' button have a realistic UI drop shadow that grounds it to the interface, rather than appearing to float unnaturally?
Are the original window reflections and bright glare from the first image clearly visible overlaying the new screen content?
Are the original fingerprint smudges and dust particles preserved and visible on top of the generated game interface?
Is the original edge vignetting (darker corners) of the screen preserved?
Does the perspective distortion of the new interface content match the physical tilt and angle of the phone screen?
Does the screen content appear emissive (glowing from within) rather than looking like a matte sticker pasted on the surface?
Is the layering order correct, with reflections and fingerprints appearing strictly on top of the UI elements?
Is the hand holding the phone rendered naturally without anatomical errors or artifacts?
Is the screen area free from severe moiré patterns or unintentional aliasing artifacts?
